code_blocks.code:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 86400 * 7, "SECONDS")
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter lowercase(action_process_image_name) = "schtasks.exe"
| filter lowercase(action_process_image_command_line) contains "/create"
| filter lowercase(actor_process_image_name) in (
    "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe",
    "winword.exe", "excel.exe", "outlook.exe")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username,
    actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line
| limit 200

scenario: Scheduled task created by a script-host parent.
snippet:
Scenario: a phishing payload or web-shell drops a scheduled task for persistence. The unusual part is rarely the task itself but its parent: a script host (`wscript`, `cscript`, `powershell`, `mshta`) launching `schtasks /create` is a notable shape.

- https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1053/005/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/taskschd/task-scheduler-start-page

Logic: combines the action (task creation) with a parent that should rarely ever schedule tasks. Tune the parent list to your environment.
